title: jasione maritima subsp. gallaecica
intro: Jasione maritima subsp. gallaecica is a subspecies of the annual flowering plant Jasione maritima, native to the northwest region of the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is also known as the Galician bellflower or marítimo gallaica. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to be beneficial for treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate digestive issues and promote overall health, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
